item.title: wasmCloud v2.0 is here
item.description: wasmCloud v2.0 is a reimagining of the wasmCloud platform: Kubernetes-native, radically simplified, and more powerful than ever.

item.title: wasmCloud at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon EU 2026
item.description: Meet the wasmCloud team at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon EU 2026 in Amsterdam!

item.title: WasmCon NA 2025 Liveblog
item.description: Meet the wasmCloud team at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon NA 2025 in Atlanta!

item.title: Introducing the next-generation wasmCloud runtime
item.description: The new wash-runtime crate features a radically simplified architecture, focusing on being the best possible WebAssembly host implementation--and paving the way for many of the major initiatives on the project roadmap.

item.title: wasmCloud at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon NA 2025
item.description: Meet the wasmCloud team at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon NA 2025 in Atlanta!

item.title: Securely Signing WebAssembly Components with Cosign (OIDC)
item.description: Build, sign, and verify WebAssembly component artifacts using Sigstore’s cosign with OIDC identity—secure, registry-native signatures for wasmCloud.

item.title: Introducing the wash CLI's new Wasm-powered plugin system
item.description: Why a Wasm-driven plugin model is so powerful for CLIs

item.title: How I wrote the GitHub Action for Wasm development with wash
item.description: Comparing approaches to authoring, maintaining, and publishing GitHub Actions.

item.title: Good first issues: Q3 2025
item.description: Whether you're a veteran wasmCloud user or brand new to the Wasm ecosystem, there are tons of opportunities for folks of all experience levels to contribute.

item.title: Q3 2025 Roadmap Recap
item.description: Recapping the major initiatives that emerged from the Q3 2025 roadmap planning meeting.

item.title: Charting the next steps for wasmCloud
item.description: Looking back and looking forward to chart the next steps for wasmCloud.

item.title: One year of wasmCloud 1.0
Platform engineering with WebAssembly and the platform harness pattern
Statement on proposed changes to CNCF NATS.io licensing and ownership
item.description: The wasmCloud core team-and our leading contributors, including Cosmonic-are steadfastly committed to open source, the needs of our community, to core tenants of software foundation membership, and to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF).

item.title: Deploy wasmCloud on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: Learn the basics of wasmCloud on AKS, including how it works, the tools you'll need to get started, and a quickstart for a simple deployment.

item.title: wasmCloud at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2025
item.description: Join the wasmCloud community at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2025.

item.title: Why we're adopting SPIFFE for WebAssembly workload identity
item.description: Explore the SPIFFE specification’s background and learn why wasmCloud is adopting SPIFFE as the standard for introducing workload identity that spans on-prem, edge, and cloud estates.

item.title: Introducing the wasmCloud benchmark chart
item.description: Learn about the Helm chart for benchmarking wasmCloud.

item.title: Adobe: Compiling C to WebAssembly components with wasmCloud
item.description: Explore a simple CRUD application in Go, compile the code to a WebAssembly component, and run it on wasmCloud using swappable, vendorless capabilities for HTTP service and key-value storage.

item.title: Walkthrough: A wasmCloud CRUD application in Go
item.description: Explore a simple CRUD application in Go, compile the code to a WebAssembly component, and run it on wasmCloud using swappable, vendorless capabilities for HTTP service and key-value storage.
